meta.voice: Brendan Franks, AZ Law Now. Lead with the 22% structural finding, not the 2024 indictment date. The fresh peg is the Feb 18 2026 Horizon segment.
meta.rules: Under 280 chars per main tweet. Identity verify built into post-x-azlaw.ts. 4-min spacing between fires.

posts.text:
AZED reviewed 385,000 ESA voucher purchases. About 22 percent came back unallowable. 84,700 transactions outside program rules.

The agency has recovered approximately $1.2M from ESA fraud since universal expansion. Per Feb 18 2026 Arizona PBS Horizon (Craig Harris reporting).
posts.reply:
Full breakdown: how the structural compliance gap connects to the named 2024 indictment, plus the 2026 ballot initiative angle.

azlawnow.com/investigations/esa-fake-children-600k-grand-jury-indictment/

posts.text:
Three former AZED ESA program specialists indicted by State Grand Jury Feb 29, 2024. Plus two of one defendant's adult children. Alleged $600K+ ring used 17 children. 5 were entirely fictitious. All 5 share the same birthday in indictment filings.

A credit union flagged the account.
posts.reply:
Mayes verbatim, plus the named defendants, plus the statutory frame (ARS 13-2310 fraudulent schemes; ARS 38-441 public officer false claims):

posts.text:
@AZAGMayes The Feb 29 2024 ESA indictment named three former AZED program specialists plus two of Sweet's adult children. AZED didn't catch Sweet's branch on its own. A credit union flagged the account. The ring ran 26 months.

The 22% unallowable rate from Feb 2026 Horizon is the structural follow-on.
